pattern: Pairing stones greedily is bounded by both the total halved and the two smaller piles, giving a closed-form maximum.
hints: Each move consumes one stone from two different piles, so the score can never exceed half the total number of stones.
If one pile is larger than the other two combined, you run out of partners and the answer is just the sum of the two smaller piles.
Otherwise the answer is the floor of the total divided by two; take the minimum of these two bounds.

edge_inputs.description: one pile dominates, limited by the two smaller
largest exactly equals sum of others

stress.description: random triples across the full range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    return [rng.randrange(1, 100001), rng.randrange(1, 100001), rng.randrange(1, 100001)]
